---
title: "Custdata_Models"
author: "MJC"
date: "2026-03-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
housing <- read.csv("06_Regression_Boston_Housing_Prices.csv") 
head(housing)
install.packages
library(caret)

library(tidyverse) 
housing$AGE <- replace_na(housing$AGE, 0) 
housing$CRIM <- replace_na(housing$CRIM, 0) 
housing$ZN <- replace_na(housing$ZN, 0) 
housing$INDUS <- replace_na(housing$INDUS, 0) 
housing$CHAS <- replace_na(housing$CHAS, 0) 
housing$LSTAT <- replace_na(housing$LSTAT, 0) 

summary(housing)
library(ggcorrplot)
# Correlation analysis between IVs and DVs
correlation_matrix <- cor(housing)
# ?ggcorrplot()
ggcorrplot(correlation_matrix, method = "square", lab = T, digits = 1, type = "lower" )

# Step 2: 
#2 . Training and Test split ----
#  Sometimes validation sample is also used.
#Use 70% data for training, 10% data for validation, 20% data for testing

set.seed(1234)
train_test_vector <- sample(c("Training", "Testing"), nrow(housing), replace = T, prob = c(0.7, 0.3))
#Create train dataset
train <- housing[train_test_vector == "Training", ]
#Create test dataset
test <- housing[train_test_vector == "Testing", ]

# Select best model statistically by using the Adjusted R-Square
lm_model <- lm(MEDV~., data=train) 
summary(lm_model)

min_model <- lm(MEDV~1, data = train)
max_model <- step(min_model, 
                  scope=(~ CRIM + ZN + INDUS + CHAS + NOX + 
                           RM + AGE + DIS + RAD + TAX + PTRATIO + B + LSTAT),
                  direction = "forward",
                  trace = 1)
summary(max_model)

colnames(train)

# 3. Cross-Validation ----
# 10-fold cross-validation. 
# 370 obs training dataset. Split it into 37 obs of 10 training samples. 
# 9*37  for first iteration and get the parameters. Then test the parameters on 10th sample. 
# repeat till all the test samples are exhausted and update the parameters. 

# ?trainControl()

training_params <- trainControl(method = "repeatedcv", 
                                number = 10,
                                repeats = 5,
                                verboseIter = T)
trainControl()
# ?train() # multiple models, linear reg, logistic, SVM, 
model_lm1 <- train(MEDV ~., 
                   data = train, 
                   method = "lm",
                   trControl = training_params)
summary(model_lm1)

library(car)
vif(max_model)
#if VIF for variable is 1, then it is not having any serial correlation with other IVs.
#5 is acceptable, 10 should be abs voided. Serial correlation= model fit issue.
# price = y-int + beta_1 * Rooms + gamme_1*Penalty 
# Penalty coefficients, SSE minimize error

#Step Function -> why use Ridge/Lasso? Multiple variables, say 13, input into model, with 80+ variables, it will error Local Minimum, 

# Ridge Regression  
ridge_model <- train(MEDV~.,  
                     data =  train, 
                     method = 'glmnet', 
                     tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 0,  
                                            lambda = seq(0, 5, length = 50)), # set alpha = 0 , lambda = 0 to 5 and set of 50  
                     trControl = training_params) 

ridge_model$bestTune

testing_Error<- predictions- test$MEDV

testing_Error<- na.omit(testing_Error)
Training_Error <- sum((max_model$residuals)^2)/nrow(train)  
Training_Error 

predictions<-predict(ridge_model,test[c(-14)])
head(predictions)
rmse_test <- sqrt(testing_Error)
rmse_test

```
